assumption_text: The civilian denominator begins with all residents still inside Marawi city at the onset because the opening hours of the seizure were city-wide, then rapidly contracts with outflow.
rationale: This avoids the opposite error of using only late-stage hostages for a battle that began with mass city-wide exposure.

assumption_text: A transitional mid-June node of 500/750/1,000 civilians is inserted between the 1 June whole-city remainder and the late-August trapped-core counts.
rationale: No daily civilian stock series exists, so an explicit transition assumption is preferable to pretending that 20,100 civilians stayed exposed through late August.

assumption_text: Government-force exposure uses 3,000 as the first clear June node and 6,500 as a late upper node from a blocked-article search snippet.
rationale: The case study's 12,000 total participants cannot be treated as simultaneous exposure, so modeled time-varying deployment is necessary.

assumption_text: The 80 male residents who may have been forced to fight are left out of civilian and military death numerators unless direct fatality evidence appears.
rationale: The prompt forbids silently reallocating ambiguous-status people into civilian or combatant totals.

assumption_text: All person-month exposure estimates use piecewise-linear interpolation and 30.4375 days per person-month.
rationale: Keeps the denominator transparent and reproducible.
